summary: I ran the repository's tests and linter against the implementer's live-status-summary changes, using only the project's standard test environment (`bin/rails test` via a runner script that works around the documented local ActionCable/Turbo boot-order load quirk) and deliberately avoiding any db:* command that could touch the shared development/production database. The full Minitest suite passed cleanly — 112 runs, 408 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 skips — and `bin/rubocop` (rubocop-rails-omakase) found no offenses across 115 files. Since the implementation builds and passes with nothing to flag, I wrote a `pass` verdict with empty findings to `.pipeliner/phases/03-build/main/test-critic/verdict.json`.
